fair_value_today.basis: base case (Metis) intrinsic value — the anchor, not a weighted blend

fair_value_12m.basis: #1 rolled forward at r=10.3% less dividend 0.0

detail.multiple_rationale: 16.7x P/E on FY2026 clean EPS of $3.59 = $59.91/share target. This is equivalent to 20x EV/NOPAT on $12.9B NTM NOPAT (EV $258B - net debt $5.4B = equity $252.6B / 4.211B shares = $59.97), confirming the two lenses converge. #4 target of ~$60 is derived from #3 ($61.24 fair value 12m) with a slight downward lean from documented judgment. Rationale for 16.7x forward P/E (vs. current 21.7x): (1) The current 21.7x already reflects ~42% YoY EPS growth from $2.53 (FY2025) to $3.59 (FY2026 consensus). If the multiple compresses from 21.7x toward 16-17x as growth decelerates post-2026, the stock converges toward $60 even as earnings grow. (2) Our base fair value today ($55.52) divided by forward EPS ($3.59) = 15.5x -- the 16.7x target is slightly generous vs. pure intrinsic convergence, reflecting that Netflix at 16-17x P/E on 13% revenue growth is reasonably valued if execution on 2026 advertising holds. (3) Per CONTRACT METHODS.md, the target is #3 leaned by documented judgment: a CONTRACTED bull moves the target UP; an OBSERVABLE bear moves it DOWN. The 2026 advertising at $3B is management-guided (CONTRACTED) and does not change our base (already incorporated). OBSERVABLE bears: BofA downgrade to Hold (Jun 16), insider selling (CLO), Reed Hastings board departure. These dampen re-rating probability. Net: lean is DOWN to 16.7x P/E = ~$60 target vs. #3 of $61.24. The gap is minimal because the evidence is balanced. The 21.7x -> 16.7x multiple compression is the structural finding: the market is pricing ~22x forward P/E on a decelerating growth story; our base says 16-17x is the honest steady-state multiple for this revenue trajectory.

street_reconstruction.what_price_implies: At 25.4x NTM NOPAT (FY2026E), the current price implies a ~9.7% earnings CAGR for 10y (then 4% terminal) at r=10.3%.
street_reconstruction.what_street_implies: The street mean target implies ~14.8% for 10y.
street_reconstruction.history_anchor: Netflix traded at 30-40x EV/NOPAT during the 2020-2021 growth peak (pre-correction), fell to 15-20x during the 2022 crash when subscriber growth stalled, and recovered to 25-35x in 2023-2025 as the password-crackdown drove subscriber reacceleration. The current 26x (EV $336B / NTM NOPAT $12.9B) is at the low end of the post-correction trading range, reflecting the Q1 2026 'beat-but-no-raise' disappointment and BofA downgrade. Historical context: Netflix at $78 is -42% from the $134 June 2025 all-time high (post-split basis). Source: market_facts.json, databundle.json.
street_reconstruction.contested_lever: Advertising revenue trajectory 2027-2030 (the crux per Theia): bears argue YouTube's 12.7% vs. Netflix's 9.0% US viewing share makes it structurally disadvantaged for advertiser budgets; bulls argue Netflix's premium, appointment-viewing environment commands premium CPMs ($40-65) that make share of viewership less relevant than CPM quality. This is the lever that differentiates base ($7.2B by 2030) from bull ($10B) from bear ($4B).

conviction.label: MEDIUM
conviction.basis: action SELL: Daedalus assigned MEDIUM (evidence read across base/bull/bear) -> MEDIUM

bridge_logic: #1 fair value today 55.52 -> #3 fair value 12m 61.24 (x(1+0.103) - div 0.0); #4 target 12m 59.95 (defensible_multiple); implied return to #4 = -23.0%, to #3 = -21.3%, to street mean 114.15 = 46.6%
